mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change versus 20-day forward returns is ≤ 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the mean return difference between high-growth and low-growth periods is insignificant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a critical threshold, it indicates labor market weakness that reduces consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mean 5-day forward return after UNRATE crosses above 4.5% is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year rolling period, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of elevated policy rates (FEDFUNDS) and declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contractionary monetary tightening that curtails economic growth and corporate profitability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS * INDPRO) in a linear regression predicting 20-day returns has a coefficient with p-value > 0.10, or the classification accuracy (directional strike rate) for negative returns under the trigger condition is below 55% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a Federal Reserve tightening cycle, increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations by raising discount rates and increasing recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mean 20-day forward return following consecutive rate hikes is not negative (t-test p > 0.05 over 20-year sample) or the predictive slope of FEDFUNDS monthly change on returns is positive in tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll expansion reflects a healthy labor market supporting consumer demand and corporate earnings, which boosts equity prices. When monthly payroll gains exceed 200,000, it signals above-trend growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The mean 5-day forward return after payroll gains >200k is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over a 10-year rolling window, or the rank IC of payroll change vs returns is below 0.03 over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in industrial production indicates rising manufacturing output and economic capacity utilization, which correlates with corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The Spearman correlation between INDPRO 3-month change and S&P 500 60-day forward return over any 10-year window is ≤ 0.10, or the positive strike rate falls below 55% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment and rising retail sales creates a 'goldilocks' scenario for the economy: strong consumer spending without overheating labor costs. This positive macro backdrop drives superior S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
regime_hint: expansionary phase with no recession signal
failure_condition: The interaction term (low UNRATE dummy * RSAFS increase dummy) in a regression predicting 20-day returns has p-value > 0.10, or the mean return under trigger condition is not significantly higher than unconditional mean over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accommodative monetary policy (low fed funds rates), increases in housing starts positively predict equity returns as they signal strength in the housing sector and broader economy. This relationship is regime-dependent and strongest when rates are low.
failure_condition: The mean 60-day forward return following positive HOUST monthly change under low-rate regimes is not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or the correlation between HOUST change and returns in low-rate periods is not higher than in high-rate periods.
